summary: Problem 9 asks for an explicit, degree-bounded, A-independent polynomial map F characterizing algebraic relations on scaled determinantal tensors Q^(αβγδ) built from generic 3×4 matrices, plus a full if-and-only-if proof. The current state has no substantive proof attempt — the single tracked issue was a placeholder seed task that was closed without any proof to evaluate, and the best-proof slot shows 100 open issues with no committed solution. The 16% solvability score reflects a genuine research-level gap with no scaffolding in place.
problems: No actual proof exists: the resolved issue was a seed/placeholder tracker, not a real attempt, so there is nothing concrete to evaluate or build upon.
Core mathematical blocker: no explicit construction of the polynomial map F on the scaled determinantal tensors has been written down, and no degree bound has been established.

The if-and-only-if (full characterization) requirement is entirely unaddressed — neither the forward (relations vanish) nor the converse (vanishing implies determinantal origin) direction has been attempted.
A-independence of the constructed relations has not been demonstrated, which is central to the problem statement.
Best-proof status reports 100 open issues with an unspecified experiment tag, indicating no consolidated or vetted solution path.
highlights: The problem is precisely specified with clear genericity (Zariski-generic 3×4 matrices), dimension (n≥5), and target tensor format (3×3×3×3), giving a well-defined target for F.
The placeholder issue was correctly triaged and closed, keeping the tracker clean of false signals.
The solvability assessment correctly identifies the key deliverables (explicit degree-bounded A-independent F plus iff proof), providing a concrete roadmap of what a complete solution must contain.

suggested_todos.title: Compute the dimension of the determinantal tensor variety and expected number/degree of relations via numerical algebraic geometry on a generic instance (n=5)
Draft an explicit candidate construction of the polynomial map F on Q^(αβγδ), starting from 3x3 minor/determinant identities of the underlying 3x4 matrices
Prove the forward direction: show the candidate relations vanish identically on all scaled determinantal tensors, independent of A
Establish and prove an explicit degree bound for the map F
Attempt the converse direction: characterize the vanishing locus and prove tensors satisfying F=0 arise from determinantal construction
Open a fresh, scoped issue replacing the closed placeholder, with subtasks mirroring the four proof components (construction, forward, converse, degree bound)
Verify A-independence symbolically on a small symbolic instance before generalizing
